summary: Target modifiable BIO-PSYCHO-SOCIAL levers to reduce numbness, cue-triggered distress, and work concentration impairment after bereavement. Levers span sleep/physiology, grief processing, emotion regulation, social support, meaning-making, mindful awareness, workplace accommodations, nutrition, and self-monitoring. Each lever maps to specific criteria (C01–C06), with measurable outcomes, feasible implementation, and safety considerations. Emphasis is on non-pharmacologic, collaborative interventions with clear measurement and a plan for progressive activation.

solutions.predictor_variable: Sleep quality, regularity, and circadian alignment
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement fixed sleep/wake times, a wind-down routine, optimize the sleep environment (dark, cool, quiet), limit caffeine after noon, and maintain a brief sleep diary to monitor patterns.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Regular sleep enhances emotional regulation and attention, reducing cue-provoked distress and improving daytime concentration.
solutions.measurement: PSQI score, average nightly sleep duration, sleep diary entries, self-rated daytime focus (0–10)
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for sleep apnea risk; avoid sedatives unless advised by a clinician.
solutions.mapping_query: Sleep hygiene predictor; map to C03 (rapid fluctuations) and C04 (work focus) via improved arousal regulation.

solutions.solution_candidate: Weekly grief-focused CBT/Meaning-Making therapy with optional EMDR-informed processing; guided journaling and meaning-oriented activities to process loss.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Active processing reduces emotional numbness and avoidance, lowers cue-triggered distress, and promotes integration of loss.
solutions.measurement: Prolonged Grief Disorder scale (PGD), frequency of intrusive thoughts, journaling engagement
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for escalating grief distress; provide supportive supervision and crisis resources if needed.
solutions.mapping_query: Grief-focused therapy targeting numbness, distress triggers, and processing (C01, C02, C06)

solutions.predictor_variable: Grounding and distress tolerance during cue-triggered distress (bio-psycho)
solutions.solution_candidate: Teach 5 grounding techniques (5-4-3-2-1, body scan) plus paced breathing and brief interoceptive exposure plans for triggers.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Autonomic arousal reduction during cues improves cognitive control and reduces moment-to-moment distress and work disruption.
solutions.measurement: Self-reported distress during triggers (0–10), weekly frequency of cue episodes, optional physiological proxies (HR)
solutions.safety_notes: Avoid hyperventilation; provide proper instruction; screen for respiratory conditions.
solutions.mapping_query: Grounding and breathing to mitigate cue distress; map to C02, C03, C04

solutions.predictor_variable: Behavioral Activation for work concentration and processing
solutions.solution_candidate: Structured daily routine with focused work blocks (e.g., 25–50 minute sessions) and scheduled breaks; include brief pleasant activities to boost engagement.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Active engagement increases mastery and cognitive control, reducing avoidance and improving focus; supports processing by re-engaging in valued tasks.
solutions.measurement: Number of completed tasks per day, daily functioning scale, self-rated focus after blocks
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor workload; adjust to avoid overload; include gradual ramp-up.
solutions.mapping_query: Behavioral activation to improve work focus and processing; map to C04, C06

solutions.solution_candidate: Identify trusted individuals; join grief support group; practice expressing needs; schedule weekly check-ins with supportive contacts.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social validation reduces numbness and isolation; supportive feedback facilitates emotional processing and coping, which can improve concentration.
solutions.measurement: Perceived social support scale, frequency of interactions, sense of validation during conversations
solutions.safety_notes: Set boundaries; ensure groups are supportive and moderated.
solutions.mapping_query: Social support & validation to reduce numbness and distress; map to C01, C05, C06

solutions.solution_candidate: Meaning-focused therapy with values assessment; 6–8 sessions plus journaling prompts; develop future-oriented goals aligned with personal values.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Meaning-making reduces existential numbness and increases engagement in daily life and work tasks, aiding processing of loss.
solutions.measurement: Grief meaning inventory, values alignment score, journaling engagement
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for persistent existential distress; refer for further support if needed.
solutions.mapping_query: Meaning-focused therapy to promote processing; map to C06, C01

solutions.solution_candidate: 8-week mindfulness program with home practice; group sessions; integrate distress tolerance and acceptance strategies (ACT components).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Nonjudgmental present-moment awareness reduces cue reactivity, improves emotion regulation, and enhances concentration.
solutions.measurement: Five Facets Mindfulness Questionnaire (FFMQ), daily practice logs, distress during cues
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for triggering content; provide supportive supervision.
solutions.mapping_query: Mindfulness to reduce numbness and cue distress; map to C01-C04

solutions.solution_candidate: Collaborate with employer to implement quiet workspace, flexible deadlines, grief leave options, and reduce unnecessary cognitive load during the acute phase.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduced cognitive load and validated workplace support facilitate concentration and grief processing.
solutions.measurement: Work performance metrics, supervisor feedback, days of leave, self-rated focus
solutions.safety_notes: Maintain confidentiality; align with HR policies; ensure reasonable accommodations.
solutions.mapping_query: Work accommodations to reduce concentration impairment and validate experiences; map to C04, C05, C06

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured dietary plan with regular meals, good hydration, and micronutrient check; monitor mood and cognitive clarity.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Nutrition affects neurotransmitter synthesis and energy, impacting mood, numbness, and attention.
solutions.measurement: Dietary recall, mood/energy logs, perceived cognitive focus (0–10)
solutions.safety_notes: Consider medical conditions and allergies; consult clinician if on medications.
solutions.mapping_query: Nutrition/hydration to improve numbness and concentration; map to C01, C04

solutions.predictor_variable: Measurement-based care through self-monitoring and feedback
solutions.solution_candidate: Daily symptom/mood/sleep/work logs; weekly review with a coach; adjust interventions based on data trends.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Regular feedback loops enable tailored, timely adjustments and reinforce engagement across domains.
solutions.measurement: Daily log completion rate, week-over-week symptom trend, trigger-symptom correlations
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure data privacy and secure storage; obtain consent for data sharing.
solutions.mapping_query: Self-monitoring with feedback to optimize targets; mapping across all criteria
